source_jobs.title: Senior Quality Assurance Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

🌎 Location and Legalities

This full-time Senior Quality Assurance Engineer position is based onsite in our Ultralytics office https://www.ultralytics.com/about in Shenzhen, China, with a hybrid model of 3 days in-office and 2 days remote.

🚀 What You'll Do

As a Senior Quality Assurance Engineer at Ultralytics, you will help ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of the Ultralytics Platform https://platform.ultralytics.com/ and our YOLO-powered workflows and APIs. Key responsibilities include:

 - Designing, architecting, and maintaining automated test suites for our backend services, web UI, and APIs using TypeScript and tools like Playwright.

 - Leading end-to-end and browser-based testing strategy for our frontend using Playwright Test

 - Writing and maintaining API and integration tests for Next.js API routes, server components, and serverless deployments on Vercel.

 - Validating data integrity and edge-case behavior across workflows that touch MongoDB, training pipelines, and inference services

 - Owning test infrastructure within CI/CD using GitHub Actions, improving test reliability, parallelization, and signal-to-noise

 - Establishing testing patterns and best practices across a Turborepo monorepo with multiple apps.

 - Mentoring junior engineers on testing strategies, code review, and quality-first development.

 - Collaborating with Engineering and Product to clarify requirements, define acceptance criteria, and catch issues early.

 - Contributing to internal QA documentation, test plans, and release readiness checks.

Your attention to detail and willingness to learn will be essential in supporting Ultralytics' mission https://www.ultralytics.com/about of delivering top-tier machine learning tools and models to the community.

🛠️ Skills and Experience

 - 5+ years of experience in QA, test automation, or software engineering, with strong proficiency in TypeScript and/or Python.

 - Deep expertise with Playwright for browser automation and end-to-end testing.

 - Solid understanding of API testing (REST/JSON), backend integration testing, and server component testing patterns.

 - Experience with modern web stacks including Next.js, React, TypeScript, serverless deployments (Vercel), and cloud infrastructure (GCP).

 - Proficiency with CI/CD systems (especially GitHub Actions), test gating, and pipeline optimization.

 - Experience working in monorepo environments with tools like Turborepo and Bun..

 - Familiarity with MongoDB and database testing strategies.

 - Proven ability to establish testing standards and mentor team members.

 - Strong problem-solving skills and a track record of improving quality processes in fast-paced environments.

 - Clear communication and the ability to collaborate across teams.

🌟 Cultural Fit - Intensity Required

Ultralytics is a high-performance environment for world-class talent obsessed with achieving extraordinary results. We operate at a relentless pace, demanding exceptional dedication and an unwavering commitment to excellence. Our team https://www.ultralytics.com/about thrives on audacious goals and absolute ownership. This is not a conventional workplace. If your priority is predictable comfort or a standard work-life balance over the relentless pursuit of progress, Ultralytics is not for you. We seek driven individuals prepared for the profound personal investment required to make a defining contribution to the future of AI.

💶 Compensation and Benefits

 - Competitive Salary: Highly competitive based on experience.

 - S

source_jobs.title: Video Editor
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

📱 Summary

Join Ultralytics as a Video Editor to craft high-impact stories that showcase real-world AI with YOLO. You’ll own end-to-end post-production across product demos, interviews, event recaps, and case studies—editing in Premiere Pro and comfortable with After Effects, to deliver platform-ready content for YouTube, TikTok, Bilibili, LinkedIn, and X. Your work will translate complex computer vision into clear, compelling narratives that educate and inspire a global developer community using Ultralytics YOLO https://github.com/ultralytics/ultralytics.



🌎 Location And Legalities

This full-time Video Editor position is based onsite in our brand-new Ultralytics office https://www.ultralytics.com/about in Madrid, Spain, with a hybrid model of 3 days in-office and 2 days remote. Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the EU, as Ultralytics does not provide visa sponsorship. Paid relocation incentives to Madrid are available.



🚀 Key Responsibilities

 - End-to-End Editorial Ownership: Lead post-production from ingest to master delivery using Adobe Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve, including light Motion Graphics and titles in After Effects.

 - Narrative Cutting: Cut diverse content—interviews, event recaps, case studies, and product walkthroughs—with crisp narrative pacing and purposeful b-roll selection.

 - AI Visualization: Collaborate with engineers to seamlessly overlay and visualize YOLO outputs (detections, masks, skeletons) and integrate clean screen recordings.

 - Multi-Platform Packaging: Prepare content for social-first distribution, with expert knowledge of codecs and delivery specs for multiple platforms.

 - Audio Finishing: Mix dialogue, SFX, and music stems for punchy, intelligible sound, ensuring noise reduction and balanced levels for broadcast-ready results.

 - Brand & Consistency: Maintain brand alignment using the Ultralytics system, and build reusable motion templates and graphics systems for speed.

 - Innovation: Experiment with AI-assisted ideation and production techniques, keeping pace with how creators communicate Vision AI.



Required Skills & Experience

 - 5+ years of professional video editing experience with mastery in Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve, plus solid After Effects skills for titling and motion graphics.

 - Editorial Expertise: Proven ability to cut long- and short-form content (interviews, demos, case studies) with strong narrative pacing and purposeful b-roll.

 - Color & Audio Post: Proficiency in color grading (LUTs, secondary corrections) and audio post-production (dialogue cleanup, EQ, noise reduction, loudness normalization).

 - Delivery Mastery: Expertise in codecs, bitrates, and delivery specs for all major social platforms.

 - AI Comfort: Comfortable visualizing AI outputs in editorial (detections, masks, poses) and integrating clean predicted screen recordings.



Nice-to-Have Skills

 - Ability to build reusable Motion Graphics templates for title systems.

 - Familiarity with lightweight scripting or automation.

 - Understanding of content that resonates with technical audiences



🌟 Cultural Fit - Intensity Required

Ultralytics is a high-performance environment for world-class talent obsessed with achieving extraordinary results. We operate at a relentless pace, demanding exceptional dedication and an unwavering commitment to excellence, guided by our mission, vision, and values https://handbook.ultralytics.com/mission-vision-values/. Our team https://www.ultralytics.com/about thrives on audacious goals and absol

source_jobs.title: LLM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

🌎 Location and Legalities

This full-time LLM Engineer position is based onsite in our brand-new Ultralytics office https://www.ultralytics.com/about in London, UK. Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the UK, as Ultralytics does not provide visa sponsorship.



🚀 What You'll Do

As an LLM Engineer at Ultralytics, you will spearhead the development and integration of advanced language model capabilities across our ecosystem. You will work on cutting-edge projects that merge the power of Large Language Models (LLMs) https://www.ultralytics.com/glossary/large-language-model-llm with our state-of-the-art computer vision https://www.ultralytics.com/glossary/computer-vision-cv technologies. Key responsibilities include:

 - Developing and scaling robust LLM-powered applications using a variety of APIs, including OpenAI https://openai.com/blog/openai-api, Anthropic https://www.anthropic.com/claude, and Gemini https://deepmind.google/models/gemini/.

 - Implementing and managing multi-API workflows using tools like LiteLLM https://www.litellm.ai/ to ensure flexibility and resilience.

 - Building sophisticated Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) https://www.ultralytics.com/glossary/retrieval-augmented-generation-rag systems, leveraging advanced techniques like embeddings with Voyage AI https://www.voyageai.com/, rerankers, and query enrichment.

 - Designing and maintaining efficient data pipelines and vector storage solutions using MongoDB https://www.mongodb.com/products/platform/atlas-vector-search Atlas Vector Search.

 - Fine-tuning LLMs on custom datasets to enhance performance for specialized tasks related to our documentation https://docs.ultralytics.com/ and user support.

 - Collaborating with our YOLO https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/yolo11/ development team to explore and build innovative multi-modal solutions https://www.ultralytics.com/glossary/multi-modal-model.

 - Automating deployment and testing processes using CI/CD pipelines https://www.ultralytics.com/glossary/continuous-integration-ci with GitHub Actions https://github.com/features/actions.

Your work will directly enhance the Ultralytics HUB https://docs.ultralytics.com/hub/ platform and empower our global community of developers and researchers.



🛠️ Skills and Experience

 - 5+ years of experience in software engineering, with a strong focus on AI and Machine Learning (ML) https://www.ultralytics.com/glossary/machine-learning-ml.

 - Expert-level proficiency in Python https://www.python.org/ and deep learning frameworks, particularly PyTorch https://pytorch.org/.

 - Proven experience building and deploying applications with LLM APIs such as OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and DeepSeek.

 - Hands-on experience with the full RAG pipeline, including vector embeddings, rerankers, and data indexing in databases like MongoDB https://www.mongodb.com/.

 - Practical knowledge of LLM fine-tuning https://www.ultralytics.com/glossary/fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and performance optimization.

 - Familiarity with MLOps principles and tools, including CI/CD with GitHub Actions.

 - A strong interest in computer vision https://www.ultralytics.com/blog/all-you-need-to-know-about-computer-vision-tasks and an understanding of object detection models like Ultralytics YOLO11 https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/yolo11/ is a significant plus.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to perform in a fast-paced, high-intensity environment.



🌟 Cultural Fit - Intensity Required

Ultralytics is a high-performance envi

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager - Mid Market
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

Following our $30M Series A, we’re scaling our customer success function out of our London hub. We’re hiring a Mid-Market CSM to own a high-volume book of business and drive retention and expansion across a diverse set of accounts running Ultralytics YOLO in production.

You’ll manage c.80 mid-market accounts representing ~$2M ARR. You will balance proactive relationship management with commercial ownership: keeping customers healthy, identifying expansion opportunities, and running renewal conversations with confidence.

This role reports to the Head of Customer Success.

👩 About the role

Own a high-volume book. 80 accounts, $2M ARR. You triage effectively, prioritise at-risk and high-growth accounts, and make sure nothing critical falls through the cracks at scale.

Drive retention and expansion. NDR is the headline metric. You hold renewal conversations, spot expansion signals; new use cases, teams, geographies; and surface them to your AE partner for joint pursuit.

Hold your own technically. You don’t need to write code. But you’ll discuss model deployment, integration patterns, and computer vision use cases credibly with technical buyers and ML teams.

Onboard and activate. You run onboarding for new mid-market customers in conjunction with Solution Engineering, driving them to first production value quickly.

Partner cross-functionally. AEs on expansion and joint account planning. SEs on technical escalations. Product on feedback loops. You are the voice of your customers inside the business.

✅ What you'll do

60 days: Understanding the value of Ultralytics YOLO for businesses. Independently running renewal calls and commercial conversations in your accounts.

90 days: Running business reviews and check-ins independently, escalating to Solution Engineering where necessary.. Renewals forecasted for the next 6 months. First expansion signals surfaced to AE partners.

180 days: Owning your book end-to-end, including renewal commercials. Tracking clearly against NDR targets. Contributing to playbook refinement and feeding customer insight back to the business. Holding technical conversations with technical buyers with authority.

🛠️ Skills and Experience

 - 2-4 years customer-facing. Technical CS, account management, or customer-facing engineer making the move to a commercial seat.

 - Technically credible. You hold a deployment conversation with an ML engineer without bluffing. You can translate technical capability into business value for a budget owner. Prior exposure to APIs, developer tooling, or ML/AI products is a strong plus.

 - Organised at volume. You’ve managed a large book before, or you’ve shown you can triage well and stay on top of a lot of moving parts without dropping accounts.

 - Commercially capable. You’re comfortable in a renewal conversation. You notice expansion signals early. You’re honest about account health; including when something is slipping.

 - Self-sufficient. There’s no onboarding specialist or implementation team behind you. You’re resourceful, take ownership, and figure things out. Solution Engineering resource is available for priority accounts.

 - Curious about the product. YOLO is the most widely deployed computer vision model in the world. Customers come in excited. You channel that and build on it.

🤝 Why this role

 - Real product pull. YOLO is the most-used computer vision model in the world. Your customers believe in the technology. Your job is helping them get value from it quickly.

 - Varied, interesting accounts. Mid-market at Ultralytics

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

Ultralytics | Hybrid - London Office | Full-time

 

Following our $30M Series A, we’re scaling our enterprise function out of our London hub, alongside teams in Madrid and Shenzhen. We’re hiring an Enterprise Customer Success Manager to own the relationship with our most strategic customers.

 

You'll own 30-40 enterprise and high-value mid-market accounts running Ultralytics YOLO in production. From contract signature through renewal — onboarding, deployment, expansion identification, and the renewal commercials. The CS function exists, the playbooks exist, and you'll plug into a rhythm that's already running. But your book is yours.

 

This role reports to the Head of Customer Success.

 

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 

Own accounts from signature. You run onboarding in conjunction with our Solution Engineers, drive from first production deployment, and stay with the customer through renewal. Front-loaded effort to get them to value, then periodic check-ins to keep them there.

 

Drive net retention. NDR is the headline KPI. You hold the renewal conversation and earn on uplift. You spot expansion signals from new use cases, to new business units or geographies and close them with your AE partner.

 

Hold technical conversations. We don’t need you to write code. But you will discuss model architecture, deployment patterns, and computer vision use cases credibly with ML engineers and technical buyers. You will be expected to understand and consult on your client’s use-cases working hand in glove with Solution Engineering where required.

 

Partner cross-functionally. AEs on joint account planning and expansion handoff. SEs on technical depth and deployment blockers. Product and Engineering on escalations and roadmap feedback. CSMs are the conduit between our customers and our business, we are looking for you to communicate effectively across our organisation.

 

WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 

60 days: Onboarded with full context on every account. Each one health-scored against the three-signal model. At-risk accounts identified with a plan for each. Able to articulate the value of Ultralytics YOLO and to run early discovery sessions with clients.

 

90 days: Running QBRs and technical reviews independently. First expansion signals surfaced to AE partners. Renewals forecasted for everything coming up in the next 6 months.

 

180 days: Owning your book end-to-end, including renewal commercials. Tracking against NDR target with clear leading indicators. Contributing to playbook refinement to show us what's working, what isn't. Providing clear feedback to the organisation on what you are seeing on the ground with our customers.

 

WHAT YOU BRING

 

3-5 years customer-facing. Technical CS or ex-customer-facing-engineer-turned-commercial. Pure SaaS CS works too. We care about outcomes, not titles.

 

Technically credible. You hold an architecture conversation with an ML engineer without bluffing. You translate technical value into business outcomes for budget owners. Prior experience with technical or developer-facing products (open source, APIs, ML/AI) is strongly preferred — we'll teach computer vision if you've done the harder work of being technical in a commercial seat.

 

You can build trust. Some of your accounts will start sceptical. You sit across from a reluctant buyer, find genuine value, and earn the relationship back. You don't oversell.

 

Commercially sharp. You hold renewal conversations confidently. You spot expansion signals early. You're honest about which accounts are healthy, slip

source_jobs.title: Lead Graphic Designer
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

🔥 Who we are

At Ultralytics https://www.ultralytics.com/, we're on a mission to simplify AI for everyone. As the creators of the world's leading Ultralytics YOLO models https://github.com/ultralytics/ultralytics, we empower millions of developers, researchers, and companies worldwide to build state-of-the-art computer vision applications. Following our $30M Series A round https://www.ultralytics.com/news/ultralytics-raises-30m-series-a, we're expanding rapidly across our global hubs in London and Madrid. This is an opportunity to join a fast-scaling, high-performance team that's redefining the future of vision AI — where ambition meets impact, and ideas become reality. We move fast. We build boldly. We execute with purpose. And we do it together. To get a feel for what we build, explore our Docs https://docs.ultralytics.com/, YOLO models https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/, and production-ready Guides https://docs.ultralytics.com/guides/.

 

👩 About the role

As Lead Graphic Designer, you’ll be the visual engine behind Ultralytics’ storytelling — turning complex vision AI into design that feels human, inspiring, and bold. You’ll own the creation of high-impact marketing and event assets across digital and print, helping our products and community stand out everywhere they appear. You’ll lead end-to-end creative production for campaigns, website and social visuals, motion content, decks, flyers, merchandise, and booth displays — ensuring every touchpoint reflects our craft and speed. You’ll also help implement our upcoming brand uplift in partnership with an external agency, translating new guidelines into scalable systems that the team can execute daily. You’ll collaborate closely with Growth, Product, and Creative to scope needs, align on priorities, and deliver work that supports adoption of Ultralytics tools — from the open-source ecosystem on GitHub https://github.com/ultralytics to the developer experience across our Documentation https://docs.ultralytics.com/ and Community https://community.ultralytics.com/. This role is ideal for a fast-moving design leader who brings structure and taste, thrives with ownership, and can ship exceptional work under real startup pace.

 

✅ What you'll do

End-to-end creative production

Own projects from concept to final delivery across digital, print, and event design.

 - Design digital campaigns, social content, and optimised web graphics for ultralytics.com http://ultralytics.com.

 - Produce presentations, announcements, and comms assets that clarify technical value.

 - Build sales and marketing collateral that supports solutions and product adoption.

 - Create event branding, booth displays, signage, and print-ready production files.

 - Deliver motion graphics and short-form video assets that elevate the story.

Design strategy & leadership

 - Partner with Growth and Product leadership to scope needs and prioritise impact.

 - Run competitive and industry research to inform creative direction and alignment.

 - Lead brand stewardship to keep our identity consistent across every channel.

Cross-functional collaboration & quality

 - Collaborate with Creative, Product, and Marketing to ship cohesive, on-brand work.

 - Own design QA to ensure accuracy, consistency, and polish before anything goes live.

 

🍌 Skills and experience

Core requirements

 - 6–8 years of graphic design experience in a fast-paced tech, AI, or startup environment.

 - A strong portfolio spanning brand, marketing, events, and multi-format execution.

 - Expert in Adobe

source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

🌎 Location and Legalities

This full-time Solution Engineer position is based onsite in our brand-new Ultralytics office https://www.ultralytics.com/about in London, UK.



🚀 What You'll Do

As a Solution Engineer at Ultralytics, you will be the technical face of our organization. You will work directly with clients to design, prototype, and deploy computer vision solutions powered by Ultralytics YOLO. You will help bridge the gap between client needs and our powerful AI tooling, guiding them through every stage from ideation to deployment. Key responsibilities include:

 - Collaborating with clients to understand their use cases and challenges, and architecting end-to-end solutions using the YOLO ecosystem.

 - Delivering high-quality technical demos, onboarding sessions, proof-of-concepts, and technical workshops that illustrate how to build with YOLO and demonstrate the power of Ultralytics tooling.

 - Designing annotation strategies and model training pipelines tailored to the client’s data, goals, and deployment constraints.

 - Debugging client issues and delivering effective solutions with a strong understanding of the YOLO package, Python, OpenCV, and model behavior.

 - Supporting pre-sales efforts by participating in technical discovery calls and helping shape proposals and SLAs.

 - Serving as a key technical advisor during customer engagements, ensuring successful integration and continued satisfaction.

 - Interfacing regularly with Sales, Marketing, Engineering, and Product teams to advocate for customer needs and influence roadmap priorities.

 - Act as a strategic technical advisor to clients, shaping computer vision solutions that drive tangible business outcomes with YOLO models.

 - Enable the sales and marketing teams with technical expertise, competitive insights, and field learnings to support campaign effectiveness and messaging accuracy.

 - Represent Ultralytics at conferences, webinars, and customer events. Contribute to technical blog posts, customer stories, and educational materials.



Your strategic vision and technical expertise will be essential in supporting Ultralytics' mission https://www.ultralytics.com/about of delivering top-tier machine learning tools and models like YOLO11 https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/yolo11/ to the community.



🛠️ Skills and Experience

 - 5+ years of experience in Computer Vision, Machine Learning, or applied AI roles.

 - Excellent client-facing skills: patient, resilient, and able to communicate clearly with technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Proficiency in Python and familiarity with the YOLO ecosystem.

 - Hands-on experience with OpenCV, PyTorch, and classical ML/CV concepts.

 - Strong understanding of end-to-end model lifecycles, including data collection, preparation, training, evaluation, and deployment.

 - Experience with Docker and comfort working across Linux, macOS, and Windows environments.

 - Demonstrated ability to teach or mentor teams and developers through courses, bootcamps, workshops, or community engagement.

 - Comfort leading workshops, writing technical guides, or presenting to developer audiences



🌟 Cultural Fit - Intensity Required

Ultralytics is a high-performance environment for world-class talent obsessed with achieving extraordinary results. We operate at a relentless pace, demanding exceptional dedication and an unwavering commitment to excellence, guided by our mission, vision, and values https://handbook.ultralytics.com/mission-vision-values/. Our team https://www.ultralytics.c

source_jobs.title: RevOps Analyst
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.


📊 REVENUE OPERATIONS ANALYST



🌍 JOIN THE TEAM POWERING THE FUTURE OF VISION AI

Hello, hola, 你好, こんにちは, नमस्ते, hallo, bonjour! Thanks for stopping by — we know your time is valuable, so here's what makes Ultralytics special.



⚡ WHO WE ARE

At Ultralytics https://www.ultralytics.com/, we're on a mission to simplify AI for everyone. As the creators of the world's leading YOLO models https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/, we empower millions of developers, researchers, and companies worldwide to build state-of-the-art computer vision applications.

Following our $30M Series A round https://www.ultralytics.com/news/ultralytics-raises-30m-series-a, we're expanding rapidly across our global hubs in London, Madrid, and Shenzhen. This is an opportunity to join a fast-scaling, high-performance team that's redefining the future of vision AI — where ambition meets impact, and ideas become reality.

We move fast. We build boldly. We execute with purpose. And we do it together.



💼 ABOUT THE ROLE


WRITE THE PLAYBOOK. OWN THE OUTCOME.

We have the foundations of a RevOps function — now we're building it out. This is a hire for someone who thrives in that phase: where the problems are real, the mandate is broad, and the impact of getting it right compounds fast.

Reporting into GTM Engineering, you'll work directly alongside leaders across marketing, sales, and customer success. We're not looking for someone who needs a playbook handed to them — we're looking for someone with the drive and judgment to write one. From day one, you'll have genuine ownership over the tools, the data, and the decisions that our go-to-market teams run on.



🚀 WHAT YOU'LL DO


REPORTING & ANALYTICS

 - Build and own the marketing reporting layer — dashboards, attribution models, and campaign performance tracking across a high-volume, always-on marketing org.

 - Keep up with a fast-moving marketing team running many programs in parallel: make sure every new initiative has measurement built in from day one.

 - Establish clear attribution across channels so we know what's actually driving pipeline and revenue — and can make smarter investment decisions.

 - Create reporting cadences and templates that give the marketing team self-serve visibility into what's working.


BUILD THE BACKBONE FOR A DATA-DRIVEN ORGANISATION

 - HubSpot is our revenue engine — you'll own it end to end: keeping the data clean, the pipeline trustworthy, and the workflows humming so our GTM teams can actually rely on what they're looking at.

 - We're heading into serious growth mode, and the foundation needs to be solid before we pour concrete — that means a proper CRM audit, a cleanup, and systems that scale with us.

 - Build the integrations and automations that keep data flowing across the stack without people having to babysit it.

 - Turn tribal knowledge into documented, repeatable processes — so the team moves faster and doesn't break things when someone's on holiday.


PERFORMANCE & REVENUE TEAM EFFECTIVENESS

 - Work directly with the heads of sales, marketing, and customer success as a strategic partner — bring data-backed recommendations, not just dashboards, and have a clear point of view on what the numbers mean and what we should do about them.

 - Own the metrics that matter to leadership: pipeline velocity, conversion rates, time to close, and the early signals that tell us where we're heading before we get there.

 - Identify what's slowing us down across the funnel — where leads stall, where deals drag — and drive the cross-functio

source_jobs.title: Commercial Associate
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.


🤝 COMMERCIAL ASSOCIATE



🌍 JOIN THE TEAM POWERING THE FUTURE OF VISION AI

Hello, hola, 你好, こんにちは, नमस्ते, hallo, bonjour! Thanks for stopping by — we know your time is valuable, so here's what makes Ultralytics special.



⚡ WHO WE ARE

At Ultralytics https://www.ultralytics.com/, we're on a mission to simplify AI for everyone. As the creators of the world's leading YOLO models https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/, we empower millions of developers, researchers, and companies worldwide to build state-of-the-art computer vision applications.

Following our $30M Series A round https://www.ultralytics.com/news/ultralytics-raises-30m-series-a, we're expanding rapidly across our global hubs in London, Madrid, and Shenzhen. This is an opportunity to join a fast-scaling, high-performance team that's redefining the future of vision AI — where ambition meets impact, and ideas become reality.

We move fast. We build boldly. We execute with purpose. And we do it together.



💼 ABOUT THE ROLE

This isn't an SDR role. It's an apprenticeship designed to turn you into an elite seller.

We don't hire people to book meetings and disappear. We hire Commercial Associates who own markets, build playbooks, and learn to close deals working shoulder-to-shoulder with experienced sellers. You'll own two strategic vertical markets end-to-end — research the territory, identify key accounts, build relationships, generate pipeline, and develop the repeatable motion that makes those verticals ours.

You'll be paired 1:1 with an Enterprise Account Executive who'll teach you how complex sales cycles actually move. This is for people who want to build something, own something, and become exceptional at commercial work.

Reporting to the VP of Sales & Success, this role is based in our London office.



🚀 WHAT YOU'LL DO


OWN YOUR MARKETS

 - Own vertical markets. You're the CEO of your territory. Research accounts, identify decision-makers, understand buying patterns, and figure out how to reach them. You'll design the motion and run it.

 - Generate qualified pipeline. Build account lists, run outbound campaigns, attend industry events, and create opportunities where none existed. Success is measured by pipeline generated and deals closed.

 - Lead on messaging. Develop the narrative that resonates in your verticals. You're building the script, not executing someone else's.


WORK DEALS & BUILD THE PLAYBOOK

 - Work deals alongside an Enterprise AE. Shadow their calls, see how they navigate complex orgs, and eventually lead discovery, presentations, and parts of the sales process yourself.

 - Build the playbook. Document what works. Turn your territory knowledge into a repeatable motion that scales.


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - 3 months: Deep understanding of your verticals, consistent outbound motion running, shadowing your paired AE on deals.

 - 6 months: Qualified pipeline generated, leading discovery calls, contributing meaningfully to deals.

 - 9 months: Verticals producing consistent pipeline, leading full discovery independently, closing smaller deals.

 - 12 months: Promotion-ready with proven ability to generate pipeline and close deals.



🧠 SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE


CORE REQUIREMENTS

 - Commercially sharp. You understand how businesses buy and how deals get done. You can have a commercial conversation with a VP without reading a script.

 - Relentlessly curious. You want to understand why buyers make decisions and what separates deals that close from deals that stall.

 - Willing to do the work. Cold calls, p

source_jobs.title: Head of Finance (Interim)
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

At Ultralytics https://www.ultralytics.com/, we're on a mission to simplify AI for everyone. As the creators of the world's leading YOLO models https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/ and the open-source Ultralytics GitHub https://github.com/ultralytics, we empower millions of developers, researchers, and companies worldwide to build state-of-the-art computer vision applications.

Following our $30M Series A round https://www.ultralytics.com/news/ultralytics-raises-30m-series-a, we're expanding rapidly across our global hubs in London and Madrid. This is an opportunity to join a fast-scaling, high-performance team that's redefining the future of vision AI — where ambition meets impact, and ideas become reality.

We move fast. We build boldly. We execute with purpose. And we do it together.



💼 ABOUT THE ROLE

As Interim Head of Finance, you'll be the financial backbone of Ultralytics during a critical stage of growth. You'll own the numbers, help shape strategy, and bring structure to a fast-moving AI company building the future of vision AI https://www.ultralytics.com/.

This is a hands-on leadership role with broad scope. Alongside core finance ownership, you'll provide operational support across legal and HR, helping the business stay sharp, compliant, and well run as we scale our products, platform, and global team.

You'll work closely with leadership across the company, translating financial insight into clear decisions and building processes that support speed without sacrificing control. This role is ideal for a commercially minded operator who enjoys ownership, variety, and building the right foundations early.



🚀 WHAT YOU'LL DO

Finance

 - Lead financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting across the business.

 - Own month-end close, management accounts, and board-level reporting.

 - Build robust financial controls, compliance processes, and reporting frameworks.

 - Manage cash flow, runway planning, treasury, and scaling unit economics.

 - Partner with auditors, accountants, tax advisors, and investor stakeholders.

Legal & compliance

 - Oversee contract management with external counsel where needed.

 - Ensure compliance with relevant regulations, obligations, and filings.

 - Own and deliver annual statutory reporting requirements.

HR & people operations

 - Support payroll operations and benefits administration.

 - Maintain HR records and local employment law compliance.

 - Partner on headcount planning, people costs, and operating structure.



🧠 SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE

Core requirements

 - Qualified accountant with ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent credentials.

 - 5+ years in finance, ideally in a high-growth tech or AI environment.

 - Experience building or improving finance processes from scratch.

 - Confidence operating as the most senior finance person in the room.

 - Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify complex numbers.

 - Comfortable working independently in a lean, high-performance team.

 - Genuine interest in AI, developer tools, and what we're building with YOLO https://docs.ultralytics.com/.

Nice to have

 - Experience supporting a Series A or B fundraise.

 - Exposure to SaaS, deep tech, or open-source business models.

 - Familiarity with employment law, HR operations, or benefits admin.

 - Experience working with legal teams on commercial contracts.

 - Knowledge of scaling tools, systems, or reporting workflows in startups.



🌟 CULTURAL FIT

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. Hello, hola, 你好, こんにちは, नमस्ते, hallo, bonjour! Thanks for stopping by — we know your time is valuable, so here's what makes Ultralytics special.

At Ultralytics https://www.ultralytics.com/, we're on a mission to simplify AI for everyone. As Product Marketing Manager, you’ll sit at the intersection of product, engineering, and go-to-market — owning positioning, messaging, and launches for the Ultralytics product suite across open-source and our commercial platform. You’ll translate real capabilities from the codebase into clear value for both developers and enterprise buyers, grounded in user feedback and measurable adoption.

You’ll lead GTM strategy for new features and releases across the Ultralytics docs https://docs.ultralytics.com/, Guides https://docs.ultralytics.com/guides/, and community channels, and you’ll build sales enablement that helps partners and commercial teams win with clarity and technical credibility.

This role is hands-on and technical: you’ll work directly with engineers and product managers to shape narratives from what we ship, validate messaging with users in the Ultralytics Community https://community.ultralytics.com/, and improve how the world understands and adopts Ultralytics.

Positioning, messaging & narrative

Developer marketing & open-source GTM

 - Partner with engineering to ship technical content tied to real workflows and code.

Sales enablement & cross-functional alignment

 - Build enablement assets: one-pagers, battlecards, demos, and objection handling.

 - Instrument feedback loops with support, community, and customers to refine GTM.


🧠 Skills and experience
Core requirements

 - 5+ years in product marketing for technical products (AI/ML, devtools, SaaS, or infra).

 - Proven ownership of GTM strategy and product launches with measurable outcomes.

source_jobs.title: R&D Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.


⚡ WHO WE ARE

At Ultralytics https://www.ultralytics.com/, we're on a mission to simplify AI for everyone. As the creators of the world's leading open-source YOLO models https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/ and the popular Ultralytics GitHub repository https://github.com/ultralytics, we empower millions of developers, researchers, and companies worldwide to build state-of-the-art computer vision applications.

Following our $30M Series A round https://www.ultralytics.com/news/ultralytics-raises-30m-series-a, we're expanding rapidly across our global hubs in London, Madrid, Shenzhen, and New York. This is an opportunity to join a fast-scaling, high-performance team that's redefining the future of vision AI — where ambition meets impact, and ideas become reality.

We move fast. We build boldly. We execute with purpose. And we do it together.




💼 ABOUT THE ROLE

As R&D Engineer — Research Role: Neural Network Architectures for YOLO — you'll design and develop the neural network architectures at the core of how YOLO https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/ is built. Your work will directly shape the open-source models used by millions of developers across detection, segmentation, pose, and emerging vision tasks.

You'll operate at the intersection of research and production, turning ideas from papers into performant architectures, rigorous experiments, and production-ready model designs. Working closely with our cross-disciplinary R&D team, you'll help define future releases across the Ultralytics ecosystem https://www.ultralytics.com/ and contribute to the technical foundations documented in our docs https://docs.ultralytics.com/ and guides https://docs.ultralytics.com/guides/.

This role is ideal for a hands-on researcher who thrives on first-principles thinking, rapid experimentation, and high ownership. You bring deep architectural intuition, strong PyTorch skills, and the drive to build original models that advance the state of the art.




🚀 WHAT YOU'LL DO


RESEARCH & ARCHITECTURE DESIGN

 - Research, design, and build novel neural network architectures for next-generation YOLO models.

 - Develop core components including backbones, necks, heads, attention modules, and efficient building blocks.

 - Read, reproduce, and extend cutting-edge papers into working model architectures from scratch.


EXPERIMENTATION & OPTIMIZATION

 - Run rigorous ablation studies and large-scale architecture experiments across standard benchmarks.

 - Improve accuracy, latency, and scaling through distillation, pruning, and quantization-aware design.

 - Build training recipes and evaluation pipelines for detection, segmentation, pose, and new vision tasks.


COLLABORATION & OWNERSHIP

 - Partner with R&D and engineering teams to turn research ideas into production-ready open-source models.

 - Contribute to foundational model initiatives that support future YOLO and broader vision systems.

 - Take ownership of assigned R&D tasks beyond architecture work and solve problems efficiently and correctly.




🧠 SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE


CORE REQUIREMENTS

 - 5+ years of hands-on experience in computer vision and deep learning with architecture design expertise.

 - Proven success building models from the ground up, not just fine-tuning pretrained checkpoints.

 - Expert-level Python and deep proficiency in PyTorch, including custom layers, modules, and training loops.

 - Strong command of convolutions, attention, normalization, optimization, and loss design fundamentals.

 - Ability to read a research paper and impleme

source_jobs.title: People Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.


💼 ABOUT THE ROLE

As People Operations Specialist, you'll be our first dedicated People Ops hire, owning the end-to-end employee lifecycle, global culture, and systems that keep our international team running.

This is a builder role, not a small-cog position in a large enterprise. You'll work directly with leadership, especially the VP of Operations, to design people processes from scratch and help scale Ultralytics from 60 to 150+ team members.

You'll build onboarding, off-boarding, HRIS workflows, employee experience, compliance coordination, and talent development systems that support our teams across London, Madrid, Shenzhen, and beyond.

This role is ideal for an astute, hands-on people specialist who brings high empathy, operational rigor, discretion, and a strong desire to shape the foundational culture of a global AI company.




🚀 WHAT YOU'LL DO


EMPLOYEE LIFECYCLE MANAGEMENT

 - Own global onboarding so new hires have the tech, tools, and context to succeed from Day 1.

 - Manage seamless, compliant off-boarding processes for departing team members.

 - Partner with regional leads and external partners on contracts, compliance, and local HR practices.

 - Serve as the go-to contact for daily people inquiries, benefits administration, and London office needs.


HRIS & OPERATIONAL EXCELLENCE

 - Act as super-user and admin for our HRIS, keeping employee data accurate and current.

 - Build, document, and streamline HR workflows that scale with our global growth.

 - Use compliant AI tools to automate repetitive HR processes and improve team efficiency.

 - Maintain people data and partner with leadership on headcount, retention, and market insights.


TALENT DEVELOPMENT

 - Support performance review frameworks, career pathways, and training initiatives.

 - Source and organize L&D resources for technical, commercial, and operations teams.

 - Help embed high-performance habits across Ultralytics' growing global organization.

 - Improve people processes through clear documentation in the Ultralytics Handbook https://handbook.ultralytics.com/.




🧠 SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE


CORE REQUIREMENTS

 - 2–4 years in HR, People Ops, Talent Ops, or a similar people-focused operations role.

 - Hands-on HRIS experience with platforms like HiBob, Rippling, Personio, or similar.

 - Strong project management skills with consistent ownership and execution.

 - High empathy, discretion, confidentiality, and maturity with sensitive employee matters.

 - Comfort using AI tools daily to streamline compliant, repetitive people workflows.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in a fast-moving tech startup, scale-up, or AI company.

 - Exposure to multi-location teams or cross-border compliance across the UK, Spain, or China.

 - Familiarity with employment contracts, benefits administration, and office operations.

 - Interest in AI, computer vision, open-source ecosystems, or YOLO models https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/.

 - Multilingual communication or experience supporting international teams.




🌟 CULTURAL FIT

At Ultralytics, we set bold goals and execute with speed, precision, and teamwork. We're driven by hard work, ambition, and resilience — building fast, learning constantly, and delivering measurable impact.

You'll thrive here if you:

 - Take ownership and deliver results with focus and accountability.

 - Combine strategic thinking with hands-on execution.

 - Value excellence, grit, and creativity in equal measure.

 - See collaboration as the foundation for meaningful progress.

Our culture

source_jobs.title: Social Media Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

🌎 Location and Legalities

This full-time Social Media Manager position is based onsite in our brand-new Ultralytics office https://www.ultralytics.com/about in London, UK, with a hybrid model of 3 days in-office and 2 days remote. Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the UK, as Ultralytics does not provide visa sponsorship.

 

🚀 What You'll Do

As a Social Media Manager at Ultralytics, you will own our digital voice and drive our community engagement strategy. You will be responsible for bringing our cutting-edge AI solutions https://www.ultralytics.com/solutions to a global audience. Key responsibilities include:

 - Developing and executing a comprehensive social media strategy to grow our audience and enhance engagement across platforms like LinkedIn, Twitter (X), Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok.

 - Creating, scheduling, and publishing high-quality, original content (including text, images, and videos) that showcases our Ultralytics YOLO models https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/yolo11/, Ultralytics HUB https://docs.ultralytics.com/hub/, and the latest advancements in vision AI https://www.ultralytics.com/blog-category/vision-ai.

 - Utilizing Adobe Creative Suite and Canva to produce visually stunning graphics and videos that align with our brand identity https://www.ultralytics.com/brand.

 - Monitoring and engaging with our community, responding to comments and messages in a timely manner to foster a positive and active user base.

 - Analyzing social media metrics using tools like Hootsuite or Buffer to track performance, generate reports, and provide data-driven recommendations for optimizing our strategy.

 - Collaborating with our engineering and marketing teams to create content for product launches, events https://www.ultralytics.com/events, and major announcements from our blog https://www.ultralytics.com/blog.

 - Staying at the forefront of social media trends, new platform features, and the competitive AI landscape to ensure Ultralytics remains a leading voice in the industry.

Your strategic vision and creative expertise will be essential in supporting Ultralytics' mission https://www.ultralytics.com/about of delivering top-tier machine learning tools and models to the community.

 

🛠️ Skills and Experience

 - 5+ years of proven experience as a Social Media Manager, ideally within the AI, deep learning https://www.ultralytics.com/glossary/deep-learning-dl, or B2D (Business-to-Developer) tech industry.

 - Demonstrable expertise in creating content for and managing professional accounts on LinkedIn, Twitter (X), Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok.

 - Advanced proficiency with Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Premiere Pro) and Canva.

 - Hands-on experience with social media management and analytics platforms such as Hootsuite, Buffer, or Sprout Social.

 - Strong understanding of social media analytics and the ability to translate data into actionable insights and strategic adjustments.

 - Exceptional copywriting and communication skills, with a talent for making complex technical topics like object detection https://docs.ultralytics.com/tasks/detect/ and instance segmentation https://docs.ultralytics.com/tasks/segment/ accessible and exciting.

 - A proactive and creative mindset with a keen eye for detail and design.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-intensity environment.

 

🌟 Cultural Fit - Intensity Required

source_jobs.title: Legal Counsel
source_jobs.description:
About Ultralytics:

At Ultralytics https://ultralytics.com/, we commit to relentless innovation in the AI space and seek team members https://www.ultralytics.com/about who resonate with our ambition to produce the world's best YOLO AI models https://ultralytics.com/yolo. If you're obsessed with AI, eager to make an impact on the world and thrive in dynamic, high-intensity environments, we invite you to apply for a position on our team.

🌍 Join the team powering the future of vision AI
Hello, hola, 你好, こんにちは, नमस्ते, hallo, bonjour! Thanks for stopping by — we know your time is valuable, so here's what makes Ultralytics special.

⚡ Who we are
At Ultralytics https://www.ultralytics.com/, we're on a mission to simplify AI for everyone. As the creators of the world's leading Ultralytics YOLO models https://docs.ultralytics.com/models/, we empower millions of developers, researchers, and companies worldwide to build state-of-the-art computer vision applications.

Following our $30M Series A round https://www.ultralytics.com/news/ultralytics-raises-30m-series-a, we're expanding rapidly across our global hubs in London, Madrid, and Shenzhen. This is an opportunity to join a fast-scaling, high-performance team that's redefining the future of vision AI — where ambition meets impact, and ideas become reality.

We move fast. We build boldly. We execute with purpose. And we do it together.

💼 About the role
Ultralytics is hiring its first in-house lawyer. There's no legal team to join - you're building it. That means setting up how contracts get reviewed, collaborating with outside counsel across jurisdictions, and deciding what processes are actually worth having at a company this size, rather than inheriting someone else's playbook.

The first priority is the part of the business that generates revenue directly: the AGPL-3.0 / Enterprise dual-licensing model behind YOLO. Every company using Ultralytics' models commercially either open-sources their entire project under AGPL-3.0 or buys an Enterprise license. Supporting compliance, converting to contract, and negotiating the Enterprise agreements themselves - is core to the P&L, not a background compliance task. We need someone who's done this kind of work before, not someone learning open-source licensing on the job.

🚀 What you'll do


Open-source licensing and IP

 - Own the AGPL-3.0 / Enterprise licensing model end to end: detect likely violations, lead outreach and negotiation to convert unlicensed commercial use into paid Enterprise agreements.

 - Structure and negotiate Enterprise License agreements directly with customers - scope, pricing triggers, audit rights, term.

 - Own IP portfolio strategy and lifecycle (trademarks, copyright, trade secrets), working with external IP agents/attorneys for filings and renewals rather than filing directly.

 - Set and maintain the open-source contribution and dependency policy - what can come in, what obligations it carries, how attribution and notices are handled.


Commercial contracts

 - Draft, review, and negotiate SaaS, licensing, distribution, partnership, and vendor agreements.

 - Build contract templates, playbooks, fallback positions, and approval workflows from the ground up.

 - Own liability, indemnity, warranty, and risk positions across customer and vendor contracts.

 - Partner with Sales, Operations, Finance, Product, and Engineering to close deals and manage legal risk.


Privacy, compliance, and legal operations

 - Advise on GDPR, data processing agreements, and international data transfers across the UK, EU, US, and China.

 - Establish proportionate AI governance and regulatory compliance processes.

 - Select and manage outside counsel across the UK, Spain, China, and other jurisdictions as needed.

 - Introduce practical systems for matter management, contract tracking, legal intake, and reporting.


🧠 Skills and experience


Core requirements

 - UK-qualified solicitor or equivalent, with genuine experience working

notes: High-tech AI company with a strong focus on engineering and product development.
